title: Public Participation Bill, 2025 (National Assembly Bill No. 44)
source: National Assembly - Justice and Legal Affairs Committee (JLAC)
body: The National Assembly's Justice and Legal Affairs Committee (JLAC) has commenced a nationwide public participation exercise on the Public Participation Bill (National Assembly Bill No. 44), 2025, in a move aimed at strengthening citizen engagement in governance and law-making.

title: Public Finance Management (Amendment) Bill, 2026
body: PUBLIC PARTICIPATION ON THE PUBLIC FINANCE MANAGEMENT (AMENDMENT) BILL, 2026 AND THE PUBLIC FINANCE MANAGEMENT (NATIONAL/COUNTY GOVERNMENTS) (AMENDMENT) REGULATIONS, 2026. The National Treasury through a Multi-Agency Taskforce has developed a Draft Public Finance Management (Amendment) Bill, 2026 and Public Finance Management (National Governments) Regulations, 2026. The Bill seeks to strengthen the effectiveness of Internal Audit and Audit Committees across Public entities in Kenya in line with the Zero Fault Audit Initiative. Pursuant to Articles 10 and 232 of the Constitution of Kenya, 2010, the National Treasury hereby invites members of the Public, Stakeholders, Professional Bodies, County Governments, Public Institutions, and all Interested Persons to submit their views on the Public Finance Management (Amendment) Bill, 2026. Written Submissions should be submitted in the prescribed Stakeholder Feedback Template by 24th May, 2026, 5.00 p.m. The Draft Bill, Regulations and the prescribed Stakeholder Feedback Template are available on The National Treasury Website.

title: Crops (Pyrethrum) Regulations, 2026
source: Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development
deadline: 14 days from the date of publication
body: Pursuant to section 5(3) of the Statutory Instruments Act, Cap 2A Laws of Kenya, the Cabinet Secretary, Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock Development, hereby notifies the public that the Crops (Pyrethrum) Regulations, 2026 have been prepared as proposed Statutory Instruments (Draft Regulations) under the Crops Act, Cap 318 Laws of Kenya. The draft Regulations are proposed by the Cabinet Secretary under the provisions of section 40 of the Crops Act, Cap 318. The proposed Regulations are to operationalize section 40 of the Act. Therefore, this public notice requests that all persons likely to be affected by the Draft Regulations submit a written memorandum on the Draft Regulations to reach the undersigned within fourteen (14) days from the date of publication in the format below. Hard copies of the written memoranda may be submitted to the office of the Principal Secretary, State Department for Agriculture.
